Applause; plaudits. shābāshī d ., v. a. To praise; applaud.

n. f. The night of record, on which men's lives and fortunes during the coming year are said to be registered in heaven. ☞ A Mah. festival, held on the eve of the 14th of Shabān, on which a vigil is observed with prayers, feastings, illuminations, and fireworks. In Hindustan lamps are lighted and prayers oftered up for the souls of deceased ancestors. 1. Har shab shab-i-barāt hai, har roz roz-i-Īd . P. Prov. Each night a festive Shabarāt, and every day as festive Id! 2. Āī shab-i-barāt, bahū sās se laṛī, Koī līpe, koī pote, koī kumhār ke khaṛī, 'Kumhār re, kumhār, haṅḍyā de baṛī! Āveṅge sab hī murde, chhoṛeṅge phuljhaṛī.' Shabbarat kī I dī . Come Shabarāt, come fights 'twixt daughter-in-law and mother-in-law! Some plaster, some whitewash, some to the potter's now withdraw, 'O potter, let me have a vessel large of earthen- ware! The spirits of the dead will come, and rockets fill the air.' shab-bedār , adj. Waking; or watching all night. shab-bedārī , n. f. A vigil. shab-chirāg , n. m. lit . the night lamp. A brilliant gem; a carbuncle. shab-khuābī, shab-khābī , n. f. Night clothes. shab-khūṅ ; H. rātā, chhāpā , n. m. A night attack with bloodshed. shab-khūṅ mārnā , v. a. To attack by night. shab-khūnī , n. f. Robbery with bloodshed. shab-khez, shab-bedār , n. m. One who rises at night for the purpose of praying. shab-khez, shab-bedār , adj. Pious; devout. shab-deg , n. f. A dish of meat and turnips cooked all night on the fire. shab-raṅg , adj. Dusky or dark-colored, black (a horse). shab-i-zafāf , The first night of the honey moon. shab-i-shahādat , n. f. The night of the 10th Moharram on which Husain was slain. shab-i-qadr ; Illit. shab-i-qadar kī rāt ; A. lailat-ul-qadr , n. f. The night of power; the 27th of Ramzān when the Qurān is said to have descended from Heaven. shab-kor ; H. ratauṅdiyā ; Panj. aṅdh-rattā , adj. Night-blind. shab-korī ; H. ratauṅdā , n. f. Night blindness. shab-i-māh , n. f. Moonlight. shab-nam , n. f. 1. Night dew; dew ( os ). 2. A very fine muslin. shab-namī , n. m. A tester. shab o roz ; H. rāt din, nis din , adj. Night and day.

The eve of the 14th day of the month Shaʻbān (on which a vigil is observed with prayers, feastings, illuminations, &c., and the Musalmāns make offerings and oblations in the names (if not to the manes) of deceased ancestors; on this night the lives and fortunes of mortals during the coming year are said to be registered in heaven)
(and gul-ě-shab-bo), s.m. (f.?) lit. 'Night-smelling,' the tuberose, Polianthes tuberosa
adj. Waking or watching all night; sleepless
or shab-parak, or shab-para, s.m. lit. 'Night-flier'; a bird of night (as a bat, an owl, &c.); see shappara
s.m. lit. 'Night-shining'; a fire-fly; a glow-worm; a gem, &c. that sparkles in the dark
s.m. lit. 'Night-lamp'; a carbuncle; a gem of the ruby kind; a sparkling or brilliant gem
s.m. 'The little night-lamp'; a fire-fly; a glow-worm
s.m. A night-attack; — shab-ḵẖūn mārnā (-par), To make a night-attack (on)
s.f. Robbery at night with bloodshed
adj. & s.m. Rising at night (to pray); pious, devout; — one who rises at night to pray
s.f. A dish of meat and vegetables (esp. turnips) dressed all night on the fire and eaten the next morning
adj. & s.m. Overtaken by night, benighted; — one who is benighted
adj. Dusky or dark-coloured, black (a horse); — s.m. Name of a horse of Siyāvush
s.m. One who walks or travels in the night; a night-watch; a thief
s.f. The night of the tenth of Moharram (on which Husain was slain)
s.f. 'The night of power,' the 27th of Ramazān (when the Qorʼān is said to have descended from heaven; — syn. lailatuʼl-qadr)
s.f. Night-blindness, nyctalopia (syn. rat-aundhā); — shab-gāh, s.f. Night-place to keep anything in; night-time
s.f. Going rounds by night, patrolling, watching at night
s.f. Nocturnal perambulation, patrolling; a procession at night
adj. Of the colour of night, dark as night, black

s.m. One who passes a sleepless night; a cricket; a nightingale; any animal that chirps or sings at night; — travelling after midnight and before sunrise; a servant that works or travels in the night-time; — early dawn; — attacking by night, night-march, nocturnal invasion
s.f. 'Night-moisture,' dew (syn. os); — s.m. A kind of fine linen, a very fine muslin
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَبْنَم (šabnam). By surface analysis, شَب (śab, “night”) + نَم (nam, “moisture”).

def: adj. Dusky or dark-coloured (a horse); name of a horse of Siyāvush.

ety: Zend shāta, perf. part. of shā, 'to rejoice,' &c.
def: adj. Pleased, delighted, exulting, joyful, happy, glad, cheerful, rejoicing (in); (in comp.) full (of); much
more: adj. Happy ( khush 1, 2). Chashm-i-mā raushan, dil-i-mā shād . P. Prov. Bright to sight, heart's delight.

adj. Pleased, delighted, glad, cheerful, exulting. شاد خوار shād-ḵẖẉār, 1. adj. Voluptuous. 2. s. f. An adultress, a strumpet. شاد دل shād-dil, adj. Cheerful, delighted, happy. شاد کام shād-kām, adj. Joyous, happy, contented. شاد کامي shād-kām-ī, s. f. Gladness, happiness, delight, content. شاد کرنا shād karnā, v. a. To gladden, to delight, rejoice, exhilarate.
subs: adj. Full of water or moisture, moist, humid, succulent; fresh, verdant; pleasant, agreeable
adj. Fortunate, well off; voluptuous; — s.f. A dancing-girl, an adulteress, a strumpet
adj. Rejoicing, exulting; joyous, happy, contented
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شاد (šād).

def: adj. = shaṭ, q.v. adj. Six
subs: s.m. The six parts of the body (viz. the arms, the legs, the head, and the waist); — the six auxiliary or supplementary branches (aṅg) of sacred science (i.e. Grammar; Prosody; Pronunciation; the meanings of unusual terms; Astronomy; and the ritual of the Hindū religion); — a set of six particular articles of offering at the first oblation of rice, &c. to the manes of deceased ancestors
and vulg. shaṭ-ānan, adj. & s.m. Six-faced; — an epithet of Kārttikeya, the son of Śiva and god of war
adj. & s.m. Six-footed; — a bee; a louse
s.m. The six seasons (see r̤itu); — shaḍ-ras, vulg. shaṭ-ras, s.m. The six flavours or tastes (see ras)
vulg. shaṭ-darśan, and shaṇ-darśan, s.m. The six systems of philosophy ( = shaṭ-śāstr, q.v.); — a man who is versed in the six systems of philosophy
s.m. The six philosophical systems of the Hindūs (viz. the Nyāya; the Vaiśeshika; the Mīmāṅsā; the Vedānta; the Sāṅkhya; and the Pāṭanjala)

s.m. pl. The six acts or duties enjoined on a Brāhmaṇ (viz. teaching the Vedas, holy study, offering sacrifices, conducting them for others, giving and accepting gifts); — the six acts allowable to a Brāhmaṇ for subsistence (viz. gleaning, accepting gifts, asking alms, agriculture, trade, tending cattle, or, according to some, lending money at interest); — six acts that may be performed by means of magical texts as taught in the Tantras (viz. killing, infatuating, enthralling, expelling, exciting animosity, and the stopping or privation of any faculty); — a Brāhmaṇ skilled in the above six acts; an adept in the Tantra magical rites
or shaṭ-konā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Hexangular; — a hexagon, any six-angled figure; the thunderbolt of Indra
adj. & s.m. Six-fold, six times; — an assemblage of six qualities or properties
vulg. shaṭ-vadan, adj. & s.m. = shaḍ-ānan, q.v.
adj. Of six kinds or descriptions; six-fold, six times; in six ways

def: adj. Joyful, glad, &c. ( = shād, q.v.); — s.m. Wine. adj. Pleased, delighted, exulting, joyful, happy, glad, cheerful, rejoicing (in); (in comp.) full (of); much
subs: adj. Full of water or moisture, moist, humid, succulent; fresh, verdant; pleasant, agreeable
adj. Fortunate, well off; voluptuous; — s.f. A dancing-girl, an adulteress, a strumpet
adj. Rejoicing, exulting; joyous, happy, contented

def: adj. See s.v. shād. adj. Pleased, delighted, exulting, joyful, happy, glad, cheerful, rejoicing (in); (in comp.) full (of); much
more: adj. Fresh; verdant; green. shādābī , n. f. Verdure; greenness.
adj. Fresh, verdant, moist, pleasant, agreeable, succulent. s. Fresh herbage.
subs: adj. Full of water or moisture, moist, humid, succulent; fresh, verdant; pleasant, agreeable
adj. Fortunate, well off; voluptuous; — s.f. A dancing-girl, an adulteress, a strumpet
adj. Rejoicing, exulting; joyous, happy, contented
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَادَاب (šādāb).

def: s. f. Freshness, verdure, moisture, succulence.
s. plur. (of شديدة) Difficult things, difficulties.
s.f. pl. (of شديدة, fem. of shadīd, q.v.), Difficult matters or things, difficulties; hardships, adversities, troubles, tribulations; disagreeable events.

ety: shād, q.v.+ān = Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. Pleased, cheerful, glad (of, -se), &c. ( = shād, q.v.). adj. Pleased, delighted, exulting, joyful, happy, glad, cheerful, rejoicing (in); (in comp.) full (of); much
subs: adj. Full of water or moisture, moist, humid, succulent; fresh, verdant; pleasant, agreeable
adj. Fortunate, well off; voluptuous; — s.f. A dancing-girl, an adulteress, a strumpet
adj. Rejoicing, exulting; joyous, happy, contented

def: s.m. See s.v. shaṭ. adj. Six
subs: s.m. The six parts of the body (viz. the arms, the legs, the head, and the waist); — the six auxiliary or supplementary branches (aṅg) of sacred science (i.e. Grammar; Prosody; Pronunciation; the meanings of unusual terms; Astronomy; and the ritual of the Hindū religion); — a set of six particular articles of offering at the first oblation of rice, &c. to the manes of deceased ancestors
and vulg. shaṭ-ānan, adj. & s.m. Six-faced; — an epithet of Kārttikeya, the son of Śiva and god of war
adj. & s.m. Six-footed; — a bee; a louse
s.m. The six seasons (see r̤itu); — shaḍ-ras, vulg. shaṭ-ras, s.m. The six flavours or tastes (see ras)
vulg. shaṭ-darśan, and shaṇ-darśan, s.m. The six systems of philosophy ( = shaṭ-śāstr, q.v.); — a man who is versed in the six systems of philosophy
s.m. The six philosophical systems of the Hindūs (viz. the Nyāya; the Vaiśeshika; the Mīmāṅsā; the Vedānta; the Sāṅkhya; and the Pāṭanjala)

s.m. pl. The six acts or duties enjoined on a Brāhmaṇ (viz. teaching the Vedas, holy study, offering sacrifices, conducting them for others, giving and accepting gifts); — the six acts allowable to a Brāhmaṇ for subsistence (viz. gleaning, accepting gifts, asking alms, agriculture, trade, tending cattle, or, according to some, lending money at interest); — six acts that may be performed by means of magical texts as taught in the Tantras (viz. killing, infatuating, enthralling, expelling, exciting animosity, and the stopping or privation of any faculty); — a Brāhmaṇ skilled in the above six acts; an adept in the Tantra magical rites

vulg. shaṭ-vadan, adj. & s.m. = shaḍ-ānan, q.v.
adj. Of six kinds or descriptions; six-fold, six times; in six ways

def: n. m. 1. The six members or parts of the body, i. e. the arms, legs, head, and waist. 2. The six supplementary parts of the Vedas , i. e. Grammar, Prosody, Astronomy, Pronunciation, the meaning of unusual terms, and the ritual of the Hindu religion.
adj. corr. of shoḍaśa, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of شدّ 'to make hard, firm, or strong,' &c.
def: s.m. Rendering hard or strong, &c.; intensifying; emphasizing; pitching of the voice (used chiefly in comp.)
subs: s.m. Emphasizing (in reading, &c.); intensity; emphasis, stress; force; severity.

def: s.m. = shaddā, q.v. s.m. The banners that are carried with the taʻziya in the Moḥarram.
more: n. m. Cor of shuhada martyrs. Banners, carried with the tāziyā , q. v.

ety: prob. corr. of A. شدة, fr. shadd, q.v.
def: s.m. The banners that are carried with the taʻziya in the Moḥarram.
more: (perhaps, from شدّ To run, to charge an enemy; or, corrupt. of شہدا shuhadā, Martyrs) s. m. The banners that are carried with the taﻌziya, in the muḥarram.

def: s.m. See s.v. shadd. s.m. Rendering hard or strong, &c.; intensifying; emphasizing; pitching of the voice (used chiefly in comp.)
more: n. m. 1. Force ( shiddat ). 2. Stress; emphasis.
subs: s.m. Emphasizing (in reading, &c.); intensity; emphasis, stress; force; severity.

def: (The reading any composition with) due emphasis.

ety: Pārsī sāṭ; old P. shiyāti; Pehl. shātish; Zend shāiti, fr. shā; see shād
def: s.f. Pleasure, delight, joy, rejoicing, festivity; marriage; a wedding; a marriage-feast
more: n. f. 1. Pleasure ( khushī , 1, 2, 4). Āë kī shādī, na gaë kā gam . Prov. No pleasure if you come, nor sorrow if you go. 2. ( shādī ) Marriage ( biyāh ). 1. Shādī/?/khānā-ābādī! Prov. Saying. A married pair good fortune's heir! 2. Shādiyān mubārak bane ko, raṅg bharī barāt hai . Wom. Marriage song. Joy joy be with the pair! a wedding party bright and fair. shādī k. , v. a. 1. See byāhnā . 2. To perform a religious ceremony previous to inhabiting a new house ( parvesh , 2). 3. To dispose of a horse. Ghoṛe kī shādī kar ḍālo. shādī-marg , n. f. 1. Death from joy. 2. An easy death. shādī h. , v. n. To be married. bī shādī, dāl chapātī ; E. bhāt kā ḍalā, nekī bīvī . Phrases to frighten children; boo! shādiyāna , n. m. 1. Music and singing at marriages or on other festive occasions. 2. Marriage fees or presents made by the cultivators to the zamīṅdār. shādiyāna bajānā , v. a. 1. To play an air…

s. f. 1. Pleasure, delight, joy. 2. Marriage, festivity, rejoicing. شادي مرگ shādī-marg, 1. s. f. An easy death, death from joy. 2. adj. Dying easily or from joy. شادي کرني shādī karnī, To feast. شاديان گنانا shadiyāṅ ginānā (dakh.) To make merry.
subs: To rejoice; to make a feast, to feast; to perform a religious ceremony previous to entering a new house; — to marry
(-kī), To get (a boy, or girl) married (to, -se)
(-kī), To marry (a child, to); — to dispose (of, — a horse, &c.)
s.f. Death from joy; dying easily, an easy death
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شادی (šâdi). Equivalent to شاد (śād) + ـی (-ī).

def: (dakh.) To marry, wed ( Harris ); to rejoice.

ety: v.r. of شدّ 'to be hard,' &c.
def: adj. Violent, strong, vehement, intense, severe, rigorous; difficult, arduous; distressing, afflicting, grievous; heavy; grave; heinous.
more: adj. 1. Heavy; grave; heinous ( sakht , 5, 6). 2. Vehement; intense. sazā-i-shadīd , Severe punishment.
(from شدّ) adj. 1. Difficult. 2. Afflicting, grievous, atrocious. 3. Violent, strong, acute, intrepid, vehement, intense.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَدِید (šadīd), from Arabic شَدِيد (šadīd).

ety: shādī+āna = Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & s.m. Relating to marriage or to rejoicings; — music and singing at marriages or on other festive occasions; a band; marriage fees or presents (made by a cultivator to the landlord, &c.)
more: adj. Relating to marriage or rejoicings; s. m. Musick and singing at marriages or on other festive occasions. شاديانه بجانا shādiyāna bajānā, To triumph.
subs: To play festive music, to rejoice; to triumph.

def: s.f. pl. (of shādī, after the model of the Ar.), 'Rejoicings'; fees paid at marriages.

ety: shād, q.v.+mān, fr. Zend aff. man = S. मन्
def: adj. Pleased, &c. ( = shād, q.v.). adj. Pleased, delighted, exulting, joyful, happy, glad, cheerful, rejoicing (in); (in comp.) full (of); much
subs: adj. Full of water or moisture, moist, humid, succulent; fresh, verdant; pleasant, agreeable
adj. Fortunate, well off; voluptuous; — s.f. A dancing-girl, an adulteress, a strumpet
adj. Rejoicing, exulting; joyous, happy, contented

def: adj. Pleased, delighted, joyful, cheerful, gay, merry, glad.
s.f. Rejoicing, joy, delight, pleasure, gladness.
s. f. Pleasure, delight, joy, rejoicing, gayety, gladness.
adj. Twenty six.
adj. Twenty-sixth.

ety: act. part. of شيع 'to be published or divulged,' &c.
def: adj. Spreading, becoming spread, published, divulged, revealed; notorious; apparent, manifest; — distressed
subs: v.n. To be spread abroad, be published, be propagated, &c.

def: n. f. 1. Increase (برکت 2). 1. Is chīz meṅ shaë nahīṅ hāi . 2. Ab to kūṛe meṅ shaë rah gaī hai . 2. Incitement; instigation. shaë denā , v. a. 1. To add fuel to the fire. 2. To instigate; incite. Shaë de-ke laṛvā dīyā . 3. See ḍhīl denā .
more: n. f. Pl. ashyā . A thing (اسباب 3—5, 7). shaë dāva, shaë mudaābihā . The thing claimed; the matter, or subject matter of a suit, shaë mubīa , The subject matter of a sale; the thing sold. shaë mutanāzea . The thing or property in dispute or under litigation. shaë marhūnā kā zabt yā qurq k . Foreclosure of a mortgage; foreclosure ( baٴebāt ). shaë makfūlā . Hypothecated property. shaë mauhūba . 1. The thing given; a dotation; present; gift; benefaction; contribution; donation; gratuity; bonus; grant; subscription; largess; endowment. 2. The property bequeathed; a bequest; legacy. shaë nā-gavār . Anything noxious, offensive, or unpleasant; a nuisance.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَیء /شَی (šay').

def: s.m. A bougie, pessary, suppository; an enema.
more: (part. act. f. from شفّ To be fine, or thin, to extenuate, to pinch as cold: Chald. To scrape, to rub, to plaster, to polish) s. m. A bougie, a pessary, a suppository.

ety: for A. شفاعة, inf. n. of شفع 'to intercede,' &c.
def: s.f. Intercession, recommendation, entreaty, deprecation
more: n. f. Intercession mediation. shafāat k. , v. a. To intercede; mediate. Pīr āp dar-māṅda, shafāat kis kī kare? Prov. The priest himself helpless, his intercession's naught.
(from شفع) s. f. Deprecation, intercession, recommendation, entreaty. شفاعت کرنا shafāʼaﻌt karnā, To intercede, deprecate.

ety: inf. n. of شفق 'to fear'; 'to be compassionate,' &c.
def: s.m. (f.?) Fear; — affection, kindness, &c. ( = shafaqat, q.v.); — the redness of the sky between sunset and nightfall, evening twilight
more: n. f. 1. The red sky at evening. 2. The evening twilight. shafaq phūnā , v. n. To appear (twilight). shafaq-i-shumālī , n. f. The northern lights; aurora borealis .
(Heb. To applaud. Chald. To suffice, to be in doubt, q. d. between day and night) s. m. 1. Evening twilight. 2. Condolence, sympathy, kindness. 3. Fear.
subs: s.f. The northern lights, the aurora borealis.

ety: for A. شفقة; see shafaq
def: s.f. Affection, kindness, benignity, tenderness, pity, compassion, mercy, clemency; condolence; favourable inclination, favour
more: s. f. 1. Clemency, compassion, mercy. 2. Kindness, affection.
subs: (-par), To show kindness (to), to take compassion (on), to commiserate
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَفَقَت (šafaqat), borrowed from Arabic شَفَقَة (šafaqa), from شَفَقَ (šafaqa). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi شفقت (śfqt).

def: n. m. An hospital; a lazaretto. shafā-khāne kī tāmīr, yā uske qāyam rakhne ke līye . G. G. For the erection and support of an hospital. shafā denā , v. a. To cure; heal. Khudā shafā de!

ety: fr. shafaq
def: adj. Of or relating to the evening twilight; like or resembling the evening twilight.
more: adj. Like, or relating to the evening twilight.

ety: for A. شفة; orig. شفہة, or شفوة
def: s.m. The lip; the mouth.
more: (Heb. ) s. m. The mouth, the lip.

def: s.m. A red peach; a nectarine.

ety: act. part. of شفع 'to join oneself to, to aid; to make petition or intercession,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Deprecating; interceding; — an intercessor, mediator, advocate, patron.
more: n. m. An intercessor; a mediator.
part. act. (of شفع Chald. To overflow, to flow out or into, to influence) Deprecating, interceding; (substantively) an intercessor, advocate, patron.

ety: rel. n. fr. shāfěʻ
def: s.m. Name of the Imām Abū-ʻabduʼl-lah Moḥammad-bin-Idrīs, one of the chiefs of the four principal sects of the Moḥammadan religion; — a follower of the Imām Shāfeʻī.
more: n. m. One of the four Imāms, and also his followers.
n. prop. 1. One of the chiefs of the four principal sects of the Muhammadan religion. 2. adj. Following the sect of Shāfiīﻌ.

ety: intens. n. fr. شفّ 'to be thin or fine,' &c.
def: adj. Very thin; translucent; transparent; clear, pellucid.
more: adj. شف was thin or pellucid. Clear; transparent ( āīnah ).
adj. (superl. from شفّ Chald. To extenuate, to polish) Transparent, clear, pellucid, very thin.

def: s.f. (fr. shaffāf), Transparency, clearness; perspicuity.

ety: v.n. fr. شفع; see shafāʻat, and shufʻa
def: s.m. Intercessor; advocate, patron; — a possessor of the right of pre-emption, one who demands and obtains, as a neighbour (or a partner), the right of purchasing a house, or a piece of land, that is to be sold
more: n. m. Intercessor; advocate; patron. shafī-i-mahshar . An epithet of Mahammad.

(from شفع) adj. 1. Advocate, patron. 2. Possessing a right of preemption founded on contiguity. (See شفعه shufʼaﻌ ). شفيع جار shafī-iﻌ-jār, The occupant of ground which is simply in the neighbourhood of another estate. شفيع خليط shafī-iﻌ-ḵẖalīt̤, The occupant of ground in actual contact, or intermixed with another estate. (The right of preemption is stronger in the latter than in the former case).
subs: s.m. The occupant of ground which is simply in the neighbourhood of another estate
s.m. The occupant of ground in actual contact, or intermixed with, another estate (the right of pre-emption is stronger in the latter than in the former case).

ety: for A. شافٍ shafiṉ, act. part. of شفو to restore to convalescence'
def: adj. & s.m. Restoring to convalescence, health-giving, healing, sanative, salutary; — relieving from doubt (an answer); categorical, decisive, positive, final; — a restorer to health, a healer
more: adj. 1. Healing; sanative; 2. Decisive; positive; final; categorical. shāfī-i-mutlaq , n. m. An epithet of the Deity.
part. act. (of شفا) Healing, sanative, salutary; a healer. شافي حقيقي shāfī-i-ḥaḳīḳī, An epithet of the Deity.
subs: 'The true or actual healer'; and shāfī-ě-mut̤laq, 'The absolute healer'; epithets of the Deity.

def: s.m. A kind of Persian character or handwriting.
more: s. m. A kind of Persian character or handwriting.

ety: v.n. fr. شفق, see shafaqat
def: adj. & s.m. Affectionate, kind, tender, merciful, compassionate; — a kind and benevolent adviser, a kind friend (syn. mushfiq, q.v.).
more: adj. 1. Affectionate. 2. Kind. shafīq' , n. m. A kind friend.
(from شفق) adj. 1. Merciful, compassionate. 2. Affectionate, kind; friend.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic شَفِيق (šafīq).

def: Mah. Wom. n. f. A bad woman.
s.m. lit. 'The rough plum'; a peach; — the bloom of the peach; the colour of the flower of the peach tree.
more: n. m. 1. A peach. 2. The bloom of the peach.
s. m. 1. A peach. 2. The colour of the flower of the peach tree.

ety: inf. n. of شغب 'to excite,' &c.
def: s.m. Noise, clamour, confusion of cries or shouts, tumult, uproar.

ety: = S. शृगाल
def: s.m. A jackal
subs: s.m. lit. 'Jackal-eater'; a term applied to a gang of free-booters on the Oude frontier.

ety: = shagāl, q.v.
def: s.m. A jackal.

ety: act. part. of شغل 'to be or become busied;' &c.
def: adj. Busying, occupying; busy, occupied, employed; attentive; intent.
more: part. act. (of شغل) Occupied, employed, attentive.

ety: cf. ćākar
def: s.m. A scholar, student, apprentice; pupil, disciple; a servant, boy, groom
more: n. m. 1. A pupil; student; apprentice. 1. Shāgird qahr, usṭād gazab . Prov. The pupil awful, but the master an incarnate fiend. 2. Shāgird rafta rafta ba-ustad merasad . P. Prov. The pupil by degrees a master grows to be. 2. A disciple; proselyte. shāgird-pesha , n. m. 1. House servants; domestics; menials. 2. The servants' outhouses or offices. shāgird-pesha ke makān , Outhouses. shāgird-i-rashīd , n. m. A favourite pupil. shāgird k. , v. a. To bind or adopt as an apprentice. shāgird h. , To become a pupil or an apprentice.
s. m. 1. A scholar, disciple, apprentice. 2. A servant. شاگرد پيشه shāgird-pesha, s. m. A servant. شاگرد رشيد shāgird-i-rashīd, s. m. A complete scholar, an apt scholar.
subs: s.m. A complete scholar; an apt scholar; a favourite pupil
(-ko), To bind or adopt as an apprentice, &c.
(-kā), To become the pupil (of); to become an apprentice (to).

Suburbs. شہرخبرا shahr-ḵẖabrā, s. m. 1. A quidnunc, news-monger. 2. An intelligencer. شہردار shahr-dār, s. m. A bird-catcher, a huntsman. شہرغريب shahr-g̠ẖarīb, s. m. A stranger, a traveller. شہرگرد shahr-gard, City-perambulator.

s.f. Disturbance of the peace of a city

v.t. To expel from the town, to banish
s.f. Wall of a city; — a prison
s.f. Wall round a town; fortifications of a town, intrenchments round a town; — shahr-pūrā, s.m. Suburbs

s.m. A city patrol; — patroning a city; a marriage procession through a city.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَهر (šahr), from Middle Persian 𐭱𐭲𐭥𐭩 (štʿy /⁠šahr⁠/), from Proto-Iranian *xšaθram (likely through Avestan 𐬑𐬱𐬀𐬚𐬭𐬀 (xšaθra, “kingdom”)), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *kšatrám, ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *tek- (“to attain”). Cognate with Sanskrit क्षत्र (kṣatrá, “dominion, kingdom”), and Parthian 𐭇𐭔𐭕𐭓 (ḥštr /⁠xšahr⁠/). Doublet of کھاتا (khātā).

ety: v.n. fr. شہر 'to be apparent,' &c.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَهر (šahr), from Middle Persian 𐭱𐭲𐭥𐭩 (štʿy /⁠šahr⁠/), from Proto-Iranian *xšaθram (likely through Avestan 𐬑𐬱𐬀𐬚𐬭𐬀 (xšaθra, “kingdom”)), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *kšatrám, ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *tek- (“to attain”). Cognate with Sanskrit क्षत्र (kṣatrá, “dominion, kingdom”), and Parthian 𐭇𐭔𐭕𐭓 (ḥštr /⁠xšahr⁠/). Doublet of کھاتا (khātā).

def: n. m. A city; town. shahr ābād k., yā basānā , v. a. To found a city. shahr-āshob , n. m. A poem on a ruined city. shahr-badar , G. G. n. 1. Banishment (جلاوطني). 2. An outlaw. shahr badar k. , v. a. To expel from the town; banish. shahr-panāh , n. f. The city walls ( chār dīvārī ). shahr-i-khamoshāṅ , Poet, lit . the city of the silent. A cemetery. Jā paṛe jis vaqt ki shahr-i-khamoshāṅ meṅ Nazīr! Nazīr. Yeh gazal, yeh rekhtā, yeh sher-khānī phir kahāṅ? When in the city of the silent, O Nazīr! you lie, Where then your lyrics', sonnets', ballads' melody. shahr shamla , n. m. A city where justice is not to be had. shahr-gard, shahr-gasht , n. m. A city patrol. shahr-gasht . 1. Patrolling the city. 2. ( Mah .) The procession of a bridegroom through the city. shah'rī , adj. Of or belonging to the city. shah'rī , n. m. A townsman; a citizen.
An exile. شـﮩر بدر کرنا shahr ba-dar karnā, To banish ( Harris ).
n. f. The great artery; the aorta .
(شه q. v. and رگ Vein) s. f. The great vein in the arm.

wety: From شہر (śahr, “city; urban area”) + ـی (-ī).

def: n. f. Opp. of dahqāniyat . 1. The polished manners of a town. 2. Civility; polish; urbanity. (دهوم)

A principal street; public road; highway. shāh-zādagī ; Pop. shahzādgī , n. f. 1. The minority of a prince. 2. Nobility ( amīrī , 2). shāh-zād/?/ ; Pop. shahzādā , n. m. 1. A prince. 2. (Wom.) God's servants sure these worshippers of Paradise cant be, Who give their lives for Hours but worshipers of lust must be. shahvat-parast ; H. kāmī , n. m. A libertine; rake; debauchee. shahvat h. , v. n. To lust after; to be in heat.

def: s.f. A thing, object; a matter, an affair; an article, &c. (syn. asbāb).

n. m. 1. A venerable old man. Ek patthar chūmne ko shekh jī kٴabe gaë, Zauq! har but qābil-i-bosah hai is butkhāne meṅ . Zauq. The Shekh to kiss a stone has to the Kāba gone, O Zauq! all idols in this world a kiss might own. 2. A chief; prelate. 3. A title taken by the descendants of the prophet and given to proselytes to Mahomedanism. 4. The first of the four classes into which Mahomedans are divided. 1. Sukh soveṅ Shekh jin ke ṭatṭū na mekh . Prov. The holy man sound sleeps who horse nor tether keeps. ( Cantabit vacuus coram latrone viator ). 2. Shekh ne kavve ko bhī dagā dī hai . Prov. The Shekh hath deceived even the crow. Shekh-chillī ; E. shekh chullī , n. m. 1. A traditionary jester. 2. (W.) A fool; madman. 3. (E.) A scamp; imp. Shekh Doṅḍū , n. m. A figure set up by way of sport or a charm to stop rain. Kabhī kahtā thā, yāro, tel ḍālo! Kabhī kahtā thā Shekh Ḍoṅḍū banāo! . Saudā. shekh'ṛā . (Contemp.) Son of a Shekh . Shekh Saddo , Wom. One of the malignant deities made responsible for many diseases, and hence an object of worship. He is said to be a great admirer of women by whom especially he is worshipped.
more: (vulg. sheḵẖ ) s. m. An old man, a venerable old man; a chief, a prelate, a title taken by the descendants of the prophet, and given to proselytes to Muhammadanism. شيخًا shaiḵẖaṉ, adv. In the manner of a chief, authoritatively. شيخ ڐونڐو shaiḵẖ-ḍoṅḍū, A figure dressed up by way of sport or charm. شيخنا shaiḵẖanā, MyLord! my preceptor! my patron!
wety: From Classical Persian شَیْخ (šayx), from Arabic شَيْخ (šayḵ).

merī śaidā ne mujhe honṭõ par bosa diyā.
My lover kissed me on the lips.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَیدَا (šaydā, “mad, insane; enamored”), from Aramaic שידא (šēḏā) or Classical Syriac ܫܐܕܐ (šēḏā, “demon”), from Akkadian 𒀭𒆘 (šēdum, “a protective deity”).

An old or elderly man, a venerable old man, an elder; a head or chief of a tribe, or of a village; the head of a religious confraternity, a doctor of religion and law, a prelate; a reputed saint; — the first of the four classes into which Moḥammadans are divided; an individual of that class; — a title taken by the descendants of the prophet; a title given to proselytes to Moḥammadanism; — sheḵẖ-ćillī or sheḵẖ-ćullī, s.m. A traditionary jester; a fool; a madman; — a scamp, rogue

s. f. The art or practice of poetry, poetry.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شاعری (šā'irī, “art of poetry”). By surface analysis, شاعر (śā'ir, “poet”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. A poetess.

ety: v.n. fr. شيط 'to be burned; to perish,' &c., or fr. شطن 'to turn away in opposition'
def: s.m. The devil, Satan; an evil spirit, an evil genius, one who leads into error or mischief; a firebrand, a mischievous imp
more: adj. شطن rebelled. Wicked; evil. shaitān ; Illit. saitān , n. m. 1. Satan (ابليس). 1. Jāhil faqīr shaitān kā ṭaṭṭū Prov. The dervise a fool but Satan's tool. 2. Shaitān ne bhī laṛkoṅ se panāh māṅgī hai . Prov. Saying. The devil even seeks a refuge from boys. ☞ The story goes that the Devil, who took a peculiar pleasure in playing with boys, one day appeared amongst them in the shape of a donkey, whereupon four boys got on his back, and a fifth, for whom there was no room, rode on a stick which he inserted in his fundament. This was too much for the Devil who forthwith vanished and forswore their company ever after. 3. Admī kā shaitān ādmī . Prov. Man's devil is man. 4. Baṛī nand shaitān kī chhaṛī, Jab dekho jab tīr sī khaṛī . Prov. My husband's elder sister is a scourge, look when you will her dart she's ready to discharge. 5. Nashā daulat kā bad-atvār ko jis ān chaṛhā, Sir pe shaitān ke…
(from شطن Heb. To be disobedient or hostile) s. m. The devil, Satan. شيطان کي آنت shait̤ān kī ānt, The devilʼs guts, any thing very long and winding (as a lane). (Plur. شياطين).
subs: To raise the devil; to make a terrible noise or row; to quarrel, fight; — shait̤ān-ćaukṛī, s.f. A company of mischievous little imps
v.n. 'The devil to get or be let loose'; the evil passions (of one's nature), to be allowed vent, &c. (see shait̤ān lagnā)
'The devil to get into (one's) head,' to be possessed by the devil; to be bent on mischief
s.m. The devil's ornament; the peacock-flower, Cœsalpina pulcherrima
s.f. 'The devil's gut'; anything very long and winding (as a lane, &c.); a long and tedious story
s.f. 'The devil's aunt'; a mischief-making woman
s.f. 'The devil's thread'; a spider's web

(-ko), To be possessed by the devil; to be in a devilish mood; to play the deuce; to practise all evil; to lust; to hanker (after forbidden things, or things not to be obtained); to have the head turned; to give oneself airs
wety: Borrowed from Arabic شَيْطَان (šayṭān), likely through Classical Persian شَیْطان (šaytān).

def: s.m. A genealogical tree; a map in which fields are marked out; a sketch, &c. in a field-book (in surveying)

subs: s.m. A genealogical table of saints or holy predecessors given to disciples; a genealogical tree
s.f. The tree of judgment (a fabulous tree which is said to have given unerring decisions on knotty points of dispute)
ex:
بلندی پر انہیں مٹی کی خوشبو تک نہیں آتی
یہ وہ شاخیں ہیں جن کو اب شجر اچھا نہیں لگتا
bulandī par unhẽ miṭṭī kī xuśbū tak nahī̃ ātī
ye vo śāxẽ haĩ jin ko ab śajar acchā nahī̃ lagtā.

At that lofty height, the fragrance of the soil does not reach
Those branches, to whom the tree no longer seems good.

s.f. A wild carrot, or turnip; the plant Asparagus racemosus.
s.m. = shaqāqul, q.v. s.f. A wild carrot, or turnip; the plant Asparagus racemosus.
v. a. To frighten, make afraid ( Harris ).

ety: S. शर्करा
def: s.f. Sugar; (met.) sweet words or speech
more: n. f. S. शर्करा, Pālī, sakkharā . 1. Sugar. 2. ( Ironic .) Dirt; filth. Tere mūṅh meṅ ghī shakkar. shakrā'na , n. m. A feast of rice and sugar. Dukhtar-i-raz se hamārā bāndh de gar tū nikāh Khūb khilvāëṅge, aë mullāh, tujhe shakrāna ham . Saudā. O priest! but wed me to the daughter of grape wine, A dainty feast of rice and sugar shall be thine. shakar-pāra , n. m. 1. A square. 2. A sweetmeat. shakkar-khorā , n. m. 1. One who lives on dainties. 2. A bird so called from its foundness for sweets. Shakkar-khore ko Khudā shakkar hī detā hai . Prov. Who sweets live on them sweets God gives. sakkar survā bolnā, shīn qāf durust na h. , v. n. To use sīn for shīn , and kāf for qāf ; to speak inaccurately.

s. f. Sugar. شکرافشاني shakar-afshānī, s. f. Scattering of sugar. شکرخا shakar-ḵẖā, Chewing sugar. شکربهتا shakar-bhatā, s. m. A kind of sweetmeat made of rice, butter, and sugar. شکرخند shakar-ḵẖand, Smiling sweetly, speaking gently with a smile of love, charming, gracious, amiable. شکرريز shakar-rez, adj. Scattering sugar, mellifluous. شکرفروش shakar-farosh, A seller of sugar; ( met. ) a mistress. شکرگفتار shakar-guftār, adj. Speaking agreeably, eloquent. شکرلب shakar-lab, adj. Sugar-lipped.
subs: s.f. The scattering of sugar; (met.) uttering of sweet words, sweet speech
s.m. A kind of sweetmeat (made of rice, butter, and sugar)
adj. Smiling sweetly; speaking gently with a smile of love; gracious, attractive, charming, amiable
s.m. One who is fond of sweets; one who lives on dainties; — name of a small bird (so called from its fondness for sweets)
adj. Scattering sugar; mellifluous; sweet or elegant (language)
s.m.f. A seller of sugar; (met.) a mistress, a sweetheart
vulg. shakar-kand, s.m. lit. 'Sweet bulb or root'; the sweet potato, Convolvulus batatas or Batatas edulis
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شکر (šakar, “sugar”).

ety: shakar, q.v.+Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: s.m. A dish consisting of boiled rice, ghī, and sugar.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شُکْرَانَه (šukrāna). By surface analysis, شُکْر (śukr, “thank”) + انَہ, from Arabic شُكْر (šukr).

ety: fr. shakar
def: s.f. A kind of phālsā so called, Grewia asiatica.
more: s. f. A kind of phālsā so called. (Grewia Asiatica).

ety: shakar, q.v. + īn = Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Sugary, of sugar; — s.m. A kind of confection.

ety: shakar + stān, qq.v.
def: s.m. A sugar-plantation; sugar-refinery; a sugar-chest.
more: s. A place where sugarcanes grow.

def: s.m. See s.v. shakar. s.f. Sugar; (met.) sweet words or speech
more: n. m. shakar-kandī , n. f. Cor. of shakar sweet , H. kand a bulb. 1. Sweet potato; Batatas edulis , Wat. Shakar-qand peṛā pāo-ser paise kā! Delhi Street cry. 2. (Slang). Membrum virile . shakar-lab , Poet. adj. Honeyed lips. shakkar-maqāl , Poet. adj. Sweet-tongued.
subs: s.f. The scattering of sugar; (met.) uttering of sweet words, sweet speech
s.m. A kind of sweetmeat (made of rice, butter, and sugar)
adj. Smiling sweetly; speaking gently with a smile of love; gracious, attractive, charming, amiable
s.m. One who is fond of sweets; one who lives on dainties; — name of a small bird (so called from its fondness for sweets)
adj. Scattering sugar; mellifluous; sweet or elegant (language)
s.m.f. A seller of sugar; (met.) a mistress, a sweetheart
vulg. shakar-kand, s.m. lit. 'Sweet bulb or root'; the sweet potato, Convolvulus batatas or Batatas edulis

def: s. m. (dakh.) Name of a small bird.
s. m. A kind of sweetmeat.

ety: for A. shakwuṉ, inf. n. of شکو
def: s.m. = shakwa, q.v.
more: (improp. shikwā ) s. m. Complaint, upbraiding. شکوه مند shakwa-mand, adj. Full of complaint. شکوه ناکي shakwa-nāk-ī, s. f. Fulness of complaint.

ety: for A. شقاوة, inf. n. of شقو 'to be unfortunate, or miserable,' &c.
def: s.f. Unhappiness, misery, wretchedness, poverty; disgrace; villainy.
more: (from شقا) s. f. Misery, disgrace, poverty, villany.

def: s.m.(?) A cleft in a mountain or rock; a cave, cavern, den; a hollow (where birds build their nests); low ground (where water stagnates).

ety: = S. शाखा
def: s.f. A bough, branch (of a tree, &c.); a twig; a stalk (of wheat, &c.); a slip or cutting; a horn; a cupping instrument; (fig.) a dilemma, difficulty; objection; obstacle; — a kind of cake made of wheat flour and fried in ghī
more: n. f. S. शाखा 1. A branch of a tree. 2. A slip or cutting. 3. A horn for cupping. 4. A stalk of wheat, etc. 5. A horn (of a deer). 6. ( farٴa ) A branch of a family or society. 7. A branch of a doctrine or philosophy. 8. A branch or part of anything. shākh-dār , adj. 1. ( Bot .) Branched. 2. Horned. shākh-dar-shākh , adj. 1. Branched out, or diffused. 2. Entangled; involved. shākh-i-daryā , n. f. A branch of a river. shākh-i-zāfrān, lit . a twig of saffron. ( Met .) A proud conceited person. Mā telī, bāp Paṭhān, beṭā shākh-i-zāfrān . Prov. shākh lagānā , v. a. 1. To put down a slip or cutting. 2. To cup ( singhī lagānā ). shākh-madrasah , n. m. A branch school.

s. f. 1. A bough, branch (of a tree). 2. A horn. شاخدار shāḵẖ-dār, adj. 1. Branchy. 2. Horned. شاخ درشاخ shāḵẖ-dar-shāḵẖ, Branched out, or diffused. شاخ زعفران shāḵẖ-i-zaﻌfarān (lit. A twig of saffron) is applied to a proud person, who estimates too highly his own worth: so, Mīr says, نازچمن وهي هي بلبل سي گو خزان هي ٿہني جوزرد بهي هي سوشاخ زعفران هي Nāz-i-chaman wah-ī hai bulbul se, go ḵẖizāṅ hai; ṭahnī jo zard bhī hai so shāḵẖ-i-zaﻌfarāṅ hai, The parterre still displays the same coquetry towards the nightingale, although the autumn is arrived; the branches, though withered, are as proud as ever. (The conceit, which depends on the yellow colour of a withered leaf and of saffron, is incapable of translation). شاخ شاني shāḵẖ shāne, is applied to various reports, calumnies, or complaints: so, Mīr again says: شاخ شاني هزارنکلين گي جوگيا اسکي زلف کا يکتار Shāḵẖ shāne hazār niklenge; jo gayā …
subs: adj. Branched out; ramified; diffused; far aṇd wide; various-coloured; various, of different sorts; entangled, involved
'A twig of saffron'; (met.) anything strange and rare; a rara avis; one who esteems too highly his own deserts, a proud or conceited person
adj. Full of branches; — s.f. A place abounding in trees; a garden

vulg. shāḵẖ-sānā, s.m. lit. 'Horn and shoulder blade'; noise, report, to-do, rumpus, row; calumny; trouble, difficulty, dilemma; objection, cavil; intimidation, threat (the phrase owes its origin to the circumstance of certain mendicants carrying in one hand a ram's horn and in the other a ram's shoulder-blade with which they make an objectionable noise before the doors of those from whom they seek relief)
(-meṅ), To raise an outcry (in the matter of), to make a pother (about); to raise a difficulty or obstacle (in respect of); to raise an objection (to), to carp or cavil (at)
v.n. A difficulty or trouble to arise; a pother to be made; &c.
s.m. A genealogical table, a pedigree
(-meṅ), To put a slip or cutting (into the ground); to graft (in); — to cup
(-meṅ), To raise a difficulty or obstacle (in the way of); to put a spoke (in the wheel of); to raise an objection (to), to carp or cavil (at).
ex: پَت جَھڑ کِی وہ شاخ اَبھِی تَک کان٘پ رَہی ہَے / وہ شاخ گِرا دو میرا وہ سامان لَوٹا دو
That branch of autumn still shakes / Let that branch fall, return my belongings
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَاخ (šāx). Doublet of شاکھا (śākhā).

def: s.m.? Hard ground (esp. at the summit, or the skirt, of a mountain); the top of a mountain; anything hard.
more: s. Any thing hard, hard ground, top of a hill.

ety: rel. n. fr. shāḵẖ
def: s.m. A yoke; the pillory.
more: s. m. The pillory, a yoke.

ety: dim. of shāḵẖ
def: s.m. A small branch, a twig.
s. m. Branch, small branch.

ety: v.n. fr. شخص 'to become raised or elevated,' &c.
def: s.m. A man's self or person; a person, being, body, individual
more: n. m. A person; individual ( ādmī , 2). Āp bhī kyā shakhs haiṅ! Well, you are a strange fellow! shakhs-i-gair ; H. an pursh , n. m. Another party. shakhs-i-sālis, tīsrā shakhs . A third person. shakhs-i-farzī ; H. mānā pursh , n. m. A hypothetical person. shakhs-i-qābiz , n. m. The party in possession; the possessor. shakhs-i-mākhūz ; H. doshit pursh , n. m. An accused person; the prisoner; one under arrest. shakhs-i-mulk-i-gair hāl sākin mumālik-i-sarkār , G. G.; H. pardesī , n. m. An alien. shakhs-i-nā-dār ; H. nirdhan pursh , n. m. An insolvent. shakhs-i-vāhid ; H. ek pursh , n. m. 1. One unassisted. 2. One person; an individual.
(from شخص To raise oneʼs self, to be borne on high) s. m. A person, body, individual.
subs: s.m. The person in possession, the possessor
s.m. A single person, an individual; an unassisted person.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شخص (šaxs), from Arabic شَخْص (šaḵṣ).

def: (شاخ and the particle سار q. d. full of branches) s. f. A garden, bower.
n. m. Cavil; calumny. shākh-shāne, yā shākh nikālnā , v. a. To carp; cavil. Har ek bāt meṅ shākhshāne nikāltā hai .

ety: for A. شخصية, abst. s. fr. شخصی, rel. n. of shaḵẖṣ
def: s.f. Individuality, personality, identity; nobility, rank, dignity, distinction, honour; humanity
more: n. f. 1. Individuality; identity. 2. Distinction; honor; dignity. Aisā na ho ki āpkī sārī shakhsīyat nikal jāë. shakhsīyat baghārnā, yā jatānā , v. a. To show off; brag.
subs: (apnī), To boast of (one's) nobility or rank, &c.; to make a display, to show off.

def: s.m. A kind of grain, Cytisus cajan.
more: s. m. Name of a grain. (Cytisus cajan).

ety: v.n. fr. شقو 'to be miserable,' &c.
def: adj. Unprosperous, unfortunate, unhappy, poor, miserable, wretched; villainous, vicious.
more: (from شقا) adj. 1. Wretched, miserable. 2. Villanous, vicious.

ety: for A. شاكٍ shākiṉ, act. part. of شکو 'to complain'
def: adj. & s.m. Complaining, querulous; blaming; — a complainer; murmurer; censurer, stigmatizer, blamer; backbiter
more: 1. part. act. (of شکو) Complaining, querulous. 2. s. m. A complainer, a backbiter censurer, murmurer.

ety: for A. شقيقة shaqīqat, v.n. fr. شقّ 'to split,' &c.
def: s.m. A crack, fissure, &c. ( = shaqq); one side (of the head), a temple.
more: (from شقّ) s. m. Temple of the head.

ety: v.n. fr. شکل; see shakl
def: adj. (f. -ī), Well-formed, comely, handsome.
more: adj. Comely (خوبصورت). Kaisā shakīl javān hai!

ety: fr. the A. shakīl
def: adj. (f. -ī), Well-formed, comely, handsome.

ety: act. part. of شکر 'to thank,' &c.
def: adj. Thankful, grateful (for, -par); praising; resigned.
more: adj. Very Ped. شکر thanked. 1. Grateful; thankful. 2. Resigned; content. Khud var shākir haiṅ .
(part. act. of شکر To give thanks; perhaps, from Heb. To hire, to pay wages or rent) adj. Grateful, praising, thankful.

ety: act. part of شقّ 'to cut or divide, to split,' &c.
def: adj. Difficult, hard, distressing, grievous, burdensome, afflicting, troublesome, severe, perplexing, embarrassing, wearisome, &c.
more: adj. See اجيرن 7, 9, 10, 11. shāq guzarnā , v. n. To lie heavy on one; to break one's heart. mehnat-i-shāqqah , Hard labour; up-hill work.
(from شقّ To split, to tear, to be difficult) adj. Difficult, perplexing, troublesome, severe.

ety: inf. n. of شقّ 'to cut, divide, cleave,' &c.
def: s.m. Splitting, cleaving, tearing, &c.; a split, rent, crack, fissure, cleft, chink, &c.; — the day-break, dawn of day
more: n. m. A crack; cleft. shaqq h. , v. n. To be torn asunder.

(Heb. To run about) s. m. 1. Trouble, labour, suspense. 2. Dawn of day. 3. A fissure, crack, rent, cleft. شق القمر shaḳḳu-l-ḳamar, Splitting of the moon. شق هونا shaḳḳ honā, To be torn, to be split, rent, &c. ¶ shiḳḳ, s. f. The half of a thing, the side of a mountain, a brother, a friend, difficulty.
subs: v.n. To be torn asunder, be cleft, be rent, &c.

ety: inf. n. of شكّ 'to be dubious,' &c.
def: s.m. (f.?), Doubt, suspense, uncertainty, hesitation, vacillation; incredulity, scepticism
more: n. m. 1. Doubt (شبه). 2. Scepticism; incredulity. shak paṛnā , v. n. To entertain a doubt. shak ḍālnā , v. a. To cast a doubt upon; call in question. shak rafa k., nikālnā, yā miṭānā , v. a. To remove a doubt. shak kā hal , Solution of a doubt. shak k. , v. n. 1. To doubt. 2. To suspect. be-shak , adv. Doubtless; certainly; I agree with you ( be-shubha ). jāë shak, muqām-i-shak , n. f. Room for doubt. kyā shak hai . No doubt; doubtless.

s. m. f. Doubt, suspense, hesitation, uncertainty. ¶ shikk, s. (in the Kāmūs thus explained, الحُلّةُ الّتِي تُلّبَسُ ظُہوُرُ اُلِسّيَتَينِ) The covering with which the backs of the two extremities of the bow are covered.
subs: (-ko), To entertain a doubt, to feel doubtful
(-meṅ), To cast a doubt (upon); to call in question
(-kā), To remove or dispel the doubts (of)
adv. Without doubt, doubtless, assuredly, certainly
'What doubt is there?' no doubt, doubtless.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَک (šak), borrowed from Arabic شَكّ (šakk).

def: (for A. شاقة shāqqat, fem. of shāqq), adj. f. = shāq, q.v.
more: (from شقّ To split, to tear, to be difficult) adj. Difficult, perplexing, troublesome, severe.

ety: rel. n. fr. shakk, q.v.
def: adj. Doubtful, perplexing; sceptical; suspicious
more: (from شکّ) adj. Doubtful, perplexing. شکي مزاج shakkī-mizāj, adj. Undetermined, wavering, hesitating.
subs: adj. Of a sceptical turn of mind, &c.; undetermined, wavering, hesitating.

ety: inf. n. of شکل 'to be a like, or match,' &c.
def: s.f. Likeness, resemblance, semblance, appearance, image, effigy (syn. shabah); model, pattern, mode, manner (syn. mis̤āl); shape, form, figure (syn. (ṣūrat); formation, configuration; (in Geom.) a diagram; a proposition
more: n. f. 1. Form ( bhes , 1-4). Jo bal kīyā to mīyāṅ kī shakal nahīṅ paichhānī jāne kī . If you are insolent I'll give you such a hammering that your own mother wont know you again. 2. Formation; configuration ( banāvaṭ , 1, 3). 3. Mode; way (اُپاے 1, 2). Rozgār kī koī shakal nikālnī chāhiye . 4. ( Math . S. साध्य, अक्रति ) A proposition; (S. क्षेत्र ) a diagram. shakal bigāṛnā , v. a. 1. To disfigure; make ugly. 2. To disgrace; dishonor. shakal banānā , v. a. 1. ( shakal denā ) To make a figure; give shape to. 2. To assume a form or character. 3. To make a wry face; to make faces. shakal se bezār h. , v. a. Not to like to see one's face. Maiṅ tumhārī shakal se bezār hūṅ! shakal-i-misālī, namūnah , n. f. A model; pattern. ham-shakal , adj. Like; similar.
s. f. Shape, form, figure, effigy, appearance, semblance, mode. شکل نويس shakl-navīs, s. m. A portrait-painter. شکل مثالي shakl-i-mis̤āl-ī, s. f. A model, a pattern.
subs: (-kī), To spoil the appearance or the form (of), to disfigure, deface; to disgrace, dishonour
(-kī), To make a likeness, or a figure, &c. (of); to give shape (to); to assume a form or appearance or character
to put on a wry face
adj. Of the same form or figure, like, similar.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَکْل (šakl), borrowed from Arabic شَكْل (šakl).

def: s.f. The vulva, pudenda mulieris; — coition.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شکر (šakar, “sugar”).

def: s.m. A plummet ( = sāhul, sahūl, sahol).
s.m. A plummet ( = sāhul, sahūl, sahol).

ety: for A. شکوَي, shakwā, q.v.
def: s.m. = shakwa, q.v.
more: (improp. shikwā ) s. m. Complaint, upbraiding. شکوه مند shakwa-mand, adj. Full of complaint. شکوه ناکي shakwa-nāk-ī, s. f. Fulness of complaint.

ety: inf. n. of شکو or شکی 'to complain,' &c.
def: s.m. Complaint; upbraiding
more: s. m. Complaint. See شکوا shakwā.
subs: or shakwạ̄-nāk, adj. Full of complaint
or shakwa-nākī, s.f. Fulness of complaint.

ety: prob. for A. شکوة shakwat, n. of un. fr. شکوٌ
def: s.m. Complaint; upbraiding
more: (improp. shikwā ) s. m. Complaint, upbraiding. شکوه مند shakwa-mand, adj. Full of complaint. شکوه ناکي shakwa-nāk-ī, s. f. Fulness of complaint.
subs: or shakwạ̄-nāk, adj. Full of complaint
or shakwa-nākī, s.f. Fulness of complaint.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شِکْوَه (šikwa), borrowed from Arabic شَكْوَة (šakwa), from شَكَا (šakā).

ety: prob. = S. शाला
def: s.f. A shawl
more: n. f. A shawl. shāl-bāf , n. m. A shawl-weaver. shāl-bāfī k. , v. a. To weave (shawls). shāl-doz , n. m. One who embroiders on shawls. shālī rumāl , A small shawl or scarf.
s. f. 1. ( शाल ) A shawl. 2. ( शाल or साल ) A common timber tree. (Shorea robusta: Roxb. ). شالباب shāl-bāb, s. m. Shawl-cloths. شالباف shāl-bāf, s. m. A shawl-weaver. See also s. سال sāl.
subs: s.m. One who embroiders on shawls.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شال (šāl). Doublet of سَاڑِی (sāṛī). Compare Punjabi ਸ਼ਾਲ (śāl) / شال (śāl), Gujarati શાલ (śāl), Marathi शाल (śāl), Kannada ಶಾಲು (śālu), Telugu శాలువ (śāluva).

ety: prob. for A. shall or shalal, inf. n. of شلّ 'to become unsound or paralyzed' (an arm, &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Having the hand luxated or withered; paralytic, palsied; languid, wearied; flaccid, soft; — a paralytic; a paralyzed or withered limb; — a kind of stained leather (inserted in the seams of shoes, saddles, &c.).
more: adj. Tired; fatigued; wearied; knocked up.
s. A sort of fine coloured leather inserted in the seams of shoes, saddles, &c. ¶ shul, adj. Soft. ¶ shil, s. A small spear, a round fruit resembling a quince.

def: adj. Excessively importunate.

ety: cf. shalḵẖ, and shalq
def: s.f. Flagellation, flogging; — coitus
more: s. f. Flagellation, flogging. شلاق خواري shalāḳ-ḵẖẉār-ī, s. f. The suffering a flogging.

def: s.f. A discharge (of musketry, &c.), a round, volley ( = shalḵẖ); — a beating (with a whip), scourging; a blow; — coitus.
more: n. f. 1. A round (of musketry, etc.); a discharge; volley; broadside. 2. Salute of guns ( salāmī ). 3. ( Slang ) A fart.
s. f. For شلق shalḳ, q. v.

ety: cf. ćhalāng
def: s.f. The springing up and down of runners or couriers by way of exercise (preparatory to a race, &c.); — a step, stride
subs: To spring up and down (as runners, by way of exercise); — to walk fast and with long strides.

def: adj. Excessively importunate.
s.m. A turnip (vulg. salgam).
more: n. m. Turnip. shalgamī , adj. ( Bot .) Turnip-shaped.

ety: fr. shāl
def: adj. Made of shawl; — s.m. A weaver of shawls and similar articles: — shālī-rūmāl s.m. A small shawl or scarf.
more: 1. ( शालि ) s. m. Rice, paddy. 2. ( शालीय ) adj. Made of shawl.

def: s.m. = shalgam, q.v. s.m. A turnip (vulg. salgam).
s.f. A discharge (of musketry, &c.), a round, volley ( = shalḵẖ); — a beating (with a whip), scourging; a blow; — coitus.
more: s. f. 1. A round, a discharge (of musketry, &c.). 2. A beating with a whip, &c. 3. Coitus. See شلخ shalḵẖ, sign. 1.

ety: inf. n. of شلخ 'to divide by a blow of a sword,' &c.
def: s.f. A round (of musketry, &c.), a discharge, volley, broadside; — sperma viri; — vulva.
more: s. f. 1. A round (of musketry, &c.), a discharge, a volley. 2. Sperma viri. 3. Vulva.

ety: Pehl. śarvār; Zend śāra-vāra; prob. akin to S. शिरस्+उरस्
def: s.m. Trousers, drawers (reaching to the feet), breeches.
more: s. m. Long drawers but not so wide as the پايجامه pāʼe-jāma (Qanoon-e-islam).

def: s.m. Syria
more: Sant. n. f. S. शम्ब 1. The iron head or ring of a wooden pestle; a mounting; a socket; the ferule of a stick or umbrella. 2. ( Mech .) A carrier. shām jaṛnā yā lagānā , v. a. To shoe or head with iron.
adj. S. श्याम Black. shām , n. m. An epithet of Krishn.

n. f. S. श्याम black. Evening ( sāṅjh ). 1. sab kī maiyā shām . Prov The shades of evening, universal mother! 2. shām ke mare ko kab tak roë? Prov. How long can you weep for one who died at evening? (Said of life-long regret). (The relations of the deceased vent loud lamentations while the corpse remains in the house. If the death happens in the morning the body is soon removed; but if in the evening it remains till next morning). 3. Shām bhaī, din chhip gayā, chakvī dīnī roë, 'Chal chakve vā des meṅ jāṅ shām kadhī nā hoë .' Night comes, the day is hid; the chakvī weeping calls, 'Come to that land, my love, where evening never falls.' 4. Khule bāloṅ mat phiro, shām kā vaqt hai . Wom. Dont go about with your hair loose, it is evening (Evil spirits will be fascinated and take possession of her). shām phūlnā , v. n. To come on (the evening twilight). shām o sahar yā pagāh ; Sant. setā ayup …

1. s. f. Evening. 2. (A) s. m. Syria. شام کي مرديکوکب تک روٴيي shām ke murde ko kab tak roʼiye, is applied to express a circumstance which is likely to be a subject of regret for life. (The expression is taken from the custom, among the relations of a dead person, of venting loud lamentations while the corpse remains in the house. If the death happens in the morning, the body is soon removed; but, if towards evening, it remains till next morning; in which case the mourners are apt to be overtaken by sleep).
n. prop. A name of Kr̤ish̤ṇa.
subs: adj. Of Syria, Syrian; — s.m. A Syrian.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شام (šām).

ety: = S. श्यामा 'night'

subs: The shades of evening to come on

(The expression is taken from the custom, among the relations of a dead person, of venting loud lamentations while the corpse remains in the house. wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شام (šām).

def: s.m. Wax (syn. mom); a wax candle; a candle; a lamp (syn. battī)
more: n. f. A lamp; candle. Shama kā pusht o rū barābar hai . Prov. The light shows the same face before and behind. (He is no hypocrite). shama-dān ; H. P. battī-dān , n. m. A candlestick. shama-rū , Poet. adj. Having a countenance resplendent as the light from a candle. shama-sāz ; H. battī-ḍhālne-vālā , n. m. A chandler; a candlemaker.

s. f. 1. A lamp, a candle. 2. Wax. شمعدان shamﻌ-dān, s. m. A candlestick. شمع ساز shamﻌ-sāz, A chandler, a candle-maker. شمع رخ shamﻌ-ruḵẖ, Red-cheeked. شمع رو shamﻌ-rū, Having a countenance resplendent as the candle.
subs: adj. Having a countenance resplendent as the light of a candle
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَمْع (šam'), from Arabic شَمْع (šamʕ). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi شَمْع (śm' /⁠śam'⁠/).
